title: Italy Beat Northern Ireland 2–0 in UEFA Playoff Semifinal (Path A)
detail: Italy defeated Northern Ireland 2–0 at Stadio di Bergamo to advance to the 2026 World Cup UEFA Playoff Path A final on March 31. Sandro Tonali opened the scoring early in the second half and Moise Kean added a second goal to seal Italy's place in the final, keeping the Azzurri firmly on course to avoid a historic third consecutive World Cup absence after missing out in 2018 and 2022. Italy will face Bosnia-Herzegovina in the Path A final on March 31 for one of Europe's final four World Cup berths.

title: Bosnia-Herzegovina Eliminate Wales on Penalties — Advance to Path A Final vs Italy
detail: Bosnia-Herzegovina eliminated Wales 4–2 on penalties after a 1–1 draw (after extra time) at Cardiff City Stadium in the UEFA Path A World Cup playoff semifinal. Wales took an early lead but Bosnia equalized to force extra time, with Bosnia ultimately prevailing in the shootout. Veteran captain Edin Dzeko and Bosnia-Herzegovina advance to face Italy in the Path A final on March 31 in Zenica — a winner-takes-all match for the final Path A World Cup berth. Craig Bellamy's Wales are eliminated, ending their 2026 World Cup hopes.

title: Sweden Thrash Ukraine 3–0 (Gyökeres Hat-trick); Poland Beat Albania 2–1 — Path B Finals Set
detail: Both UEFA Path B World Cup playoff semifinals produced decisive results on March 26. Sweden demolished Ukraine 3–0 in Valencia as Arsenal striker Viktor Gyökeres scored a stunning hat-trick to send Sweden into the Path B final. Poland defeated Albania 2–1 in Warsaw to also advance. Sweden and Poland meet on March 31 in Solna (Friends Arena) for the Path B World Cup berth. Ukraine — who had played much of their qualifying campaign as a war-displaced nation — are eliminated from World Cup contention.

title: Turkey and Kosovo Advance to Path C Final — Kosovo Stun Slovakia 4–3 in Biggest Upset
detail: Two dramatic UEFA Path C World Cup playoff semifinals on March 26 set up a Turkey vs. Kosovo final on March 31. Turkey defeated Romania 1–0 in Istanbul, with Ferdi Kadioglu's 53rd-minute goal — set up by Real Madrid's Arda Güler — proving the difference. In the day's biggest shock, Kosovo defeated Slovakia 4–3 in a thrilling comeback. Kosovo — the lowest-seeded team in the European playoffs, ranked 79th — eliminated a Slovakia side that had conceded no goals in all three home qualifying matches. Turkey and Kosovo face each other on March 31 in Pristina for the Path C World Cup berth.

title: Denmark Rout North Macedonia 4–0; Czechia Edge Ireland on Penalties — Path D Finals Set
detail: Denmark and Czechia both advanced from the UEFA Path D World Cup playoff semifinals on March 26. Denmark thrashed North Macedonia 4–0 in Copenhagen — Christian Eriksen and Rasmus Højlund among the stars as Denmark delivered their most dominant performance of the qualifying cycle. Czechia defeated the Republic of Ireland 4–2 on penalties after a 2–2 draw (after extra time) in Prague. Denmark and Czechia meet in the Path D final on March 31 in Prague — with the winner joining Group A alongside co-host Mexico, South Africa, and South Korea at the 2026 World Cup.

title: Bolivia Beat Suriname 2–1 at Estadio BBVA — Advance to Intercontinental Final vs Iraq
detail: Bolivia defeated Suriname 2–1 at Estadio BBVA in Monterrey in the FIFA World Cup 2026 intercontinental play-off Pathway 2 semifinal. Bolivia — who had stunningly beaten Brazil 1–0 during CONMEBOL qualifying — opened the scoring through Paniagua in the 72nd minute before striker Miguel Terceros converted a penalty in the 79th minute to seal the result. Suriname pulled a consolation goal back but could not complete a comeback. Bolivia advance to face Iraq on March 31 in the Pathway 2 intercontinental final, with the winner earning a berth in Group I alongside France, Senegal, and Norway.

title: Jamaica Beat New Caledonia 1–0 in Guadalajara — Advance to Intercontinental Final vs DR Congo
detail: Jamaica defeated New Caledonia 1–0 at Estadio Guadalajara (Estadio Akron) in Guadalajara to advance to the FIFA World Cup 2026 intercontinental Pathway 1 final. A first-half goal from Bailey Cadamarteri proved decisive as Jamaica — the strong favourites — avoided an upset by the Oceania representatives. Jamaica now face DR Congo in the Pathway 1 final on March 31, with the winner claiming one of the last two berths in the 48-team World Cup field. The Pathway 1 winner will join Group H alongside Spain, Morocco, and South Africa.

title: FIFA Announces Last-Minute Ticket Sales Phase Opens April 1
detail: FIFA confirmed on March 26 that the final last-minute ticket sales phase for the 2026 World Cup will begin on April 1, 2026, on a first-come, first-served basis. More than one million tickets were sold during previous sales windows, which closed in February. FIFA noted unprecedented demand with over 500 million ticket applications received across all sales phases. The announcement came amid widespread fan criticism of dynamic pricing that has seen Final tickets reach $8,680 face value and $143,750 on FIFA's own official resale platform — 41 times face value.
